speaker: BOYET
text: Now, madam, summon up your dearest spirits.

speaker: BOYET
text: Consider who the King your father sends,

speaker: BOYET
text: To whom he sends, and what’s his embassy.

speaker: BOYET
text: Yourself, held precious in the world’s esteem,

speaker: BOYET
text: To parley with the sole inheritor

speaker: BOYET
text: Of all perfections that a man may owe,

speaker: BOYET
text: Matchless Navarre; the plea of no less weight

speaker: BOYET
text: Than Aquitaine, a dowry for a queen.

speaker: BOYET
text: Be now as prodigal of all dear grace

speaker: BOYET
text: As nature was in making graces dear

speaker: BOYET
text: When she did starve the general world besides

speaker: BOYET
text: And prodigally gave them all to you.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Good Lord Boyet, my beauty, though but mean,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Needs not the painted flourish of your praise.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Beauty is bought by judgment of the eye,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Not uttered by base sale of chapmen’s tongues.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I am less proud to hear you tell my worth

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Than you much willing to be counted wise

speaker: PRINCESS
text: In spending your wit in the praise of mine.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But now to task the tasker: good Boyet,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: You are not ignorant all-telling fame

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Doth noise abroad Navarre hath made a vow,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Till painful study shall outwear three years,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: No woman may approach his silent court.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Therefore to ’s seemeth it a needful course,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Before we enter his forbidden gates,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: To know his pleasure, and in that behalf,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Bold of your worthiness, we single you

speaker: PRINCESS
text: As our best-moving fair solicitor.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Tell him the daughter of the King of France

speaker: PRINCESS
text: On serious business craving quick dispatch,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Importunes personal conference with his Grace.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Haste, signify so much, while we attend,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Like humble-visaged suitors, his high will.

speaker: BOYET
text: Proud of employment, willingly I go.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: All pride is willing pride, and yours is so.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Who are the votaries, my loving lords,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: That are vow-fellows with this virtuous duke?

speaker:
A
               
              LORD
text: Lord Longaville is one.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Know you the man?

speaker: MARIA
text: I know him, madam. At a marriage feast

speaker: MARIA
text: Between Lord Perigort and the beauteous heir

speaker: MARIA
text: Of Jaques Falconbridge, solemnizèd

speaker: MARIA
text: In Normandy, saw I this Longaville.

speaker: MARIA
text: A man of sovereign parts he is esteemed,

speaker: MARIA
text: Well fitted in arts, glorious in arms.

speaker: MARIA
text: Nothing becomes him ill that he would well.

speaker: MARIA
text: The only soil of his fair virtue’s gloss,

speaker: MARIA
text: If virtue’s gloss will stain with any soil,

speaker: MARIA
text: Is a sharp wit matched with too blunt a will,

speaker: MARIA
text: Whose edge hath power to cut, whose will still wills

speaker: MARIA
text: It should none spare that come within his power.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Some merry mocking lord, belike. Is ’t so?

speaker: MARIA
text: They say so most that most his humors know.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Such short-lived wits do wither as they grow.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Who are the rest?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: The young Dumaine, a well-accomplished youth,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Of all that virtue love for virtue loved.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Most power to do most harm, least knowing ill;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: For he hath wit to make an ill shape good,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And shape to win grace though he had no wit.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I saw him at the Duke Alanson’s once,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And much too little of that good I saw

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Is my report to his great worthiness.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Another of these students at that time

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Was there with him, if I have heard a truth.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Berowne they call him, but a merrier man,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Within the limit of becoming mirth,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: I never spent an hour’s talk withal.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: His eye begets occasion for his wit,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: For every object that the one doth catch

speaker: ROSALINE
text: The other turns to a mirth-moving jest,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Which his fair tongue, conceit’s expositor,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Delivers in such apt and gracious words

speaker: ROSALINE
text: That agèd ears play truant at his tales,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And younger hearings are quite ravishèd,

speaker: ROSALINE
text: So sweet and voluble is his discourse.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: God bless my ladies, are they all in love,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: That every one her own hath garnishèd

speaker: PRINCESS
text: With such bedecking ornaments of praise?

speaker:
A
               
              LORD
text: Here comes Boyet.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Now, what admittance, lord?

speaker: BOYET
text: Navarre had notice of your fair approach,

speaker: BOYET
text: And he and his competitors in oath

speaker: BOYET
text: Were all addressed to meet you, gentle lady,

speaker: BOYET
text: Before I came. Marry, thus much I have learned:

speaker: BOYET
text: He rather means to lodge you in the field,

speaker: BOYET
text: Like one that comes here to besiege his court,

speaker: BOYET
text: Than seek a dispensation for his oath

speaker: BOYET
text: To let you enter his unpeopled house.

speaker: BOYET
text: Here comes Navarre.

speaker: KING
text: Fair Princess, welcome to the court of Navarre.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: “Fair” I give you back again, and “welcome”

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I have not yet. The roof of this court is too

speaker: PRINCESS
text: high to be yours, and welcome to the wide fields too

speaker: PRINCESS
text: base to be mine.

speaker: KING
text: You shall be welcome, madam, to my court.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I will be welcome, then. Conduct me thither.

speaker: KING
text: Hear me, dear lady. I have sworn an oath.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Our Lady help my lord! He’ll be forsworn.

speaker: KING
text: Not for the world, fair madam, by my will.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Why, will shall break it, will and nothing else.

speaker: KING
text: Your Ladyship is ignorant what it is.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Were my lord so, his ignorance were wise,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Where now his knowledge must prove ignorance.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: I hear your Grace hath sworn out housekeeping.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: ’Tis deadly sin to keep that oath, my lord,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And sin to break it.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: But pardon me, I am too sudden bold.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: To teach a teacher ill beseemeth me.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Vouchsafe to read the purpose of my coming,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And suddenly resolve me in my suit.

speaker: KING
text: Madam, I will, if suddenly I may.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: You will the sooner that I were away,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: For you’ll prove perjured if you make me stay.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Did not I dance with you in Brabant once?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Did not I dance with you in Brabant once?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I know you did.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: How needless was it then

speaker: ROSALINE
text: To ask the question.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: You must not be so quick.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: ’Tis long of you that spur me with such questions.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Your wit’s too hot, it speeds too fast; ’twill tire.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Not till it leave the rider in the mire.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: What time o’ day?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: The hour that fools should ask.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Now fair befall your mask.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Fair fall the face it covers.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: And send you many lovers.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Amen, so you be none.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Nay, then, will I be gone.

speaker: KING
text: Madam, your father here doth intimate

speaker: KING
text: The payment of a hundred thousand crowns,

speaker: KING
text: Being but the one half of an entire sum

speaker: KING
text: Disbursèd by my father in his wars.

speaker: KING
text: But say that he or we, as neither have,

speaker: KING
text: Received that sum, yet there remains unpaid

speaker: KING
text: A hundred thousand more, in surety of the which

speaker: KING
text: One part of Aquitaine is bound to us,

speaker: KING
text: Although not valued to the money’s worth.

speaker: KING
text: If then the King your father will restore

speaker: KING
text: But that one half which is unsatisfied,

speaker: KING
text: We will give up our right in Aquitaine,

speaker: KING
text: And hold fair friendship with his Majesty.

speaker: KING
text: But that, it seems, he little purposeth;

speaker: KING
text: For here he doth demand to have repaid

speaker: KING
text: A hundred thousand crowns, and not demands,

speaker: KING
text: On payment of a hundred thousand crowns,

speaker: KING
text: To have his title live in Aquitaine—

speaker: KING
text: Which we much rather had depart withal,

speaker: KING
text: And have the money by our father lent,

speaker: KING
text: Than Aquitaine, so gelded as it is.

speaker: KING
text: Dear Princess, were not his requests so far

speaker: KING
text: From reason’s yielding, your fair self should make

speaker: KING
text: A yielding ’gainst some reason in my breast,

speaker: KING
text: And go well satisfied to France again.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: You do the King my father too much wrong,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: And wrong the reputation of your name,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: In so unseeming to confess receipt

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Of that which hath so faithfully been paid.

speaker: KING
text: I do protest I never heard of it;

speaker: KING
text: And if you prove it, I’ll repay it back

speaker: KING
text: Or yield up Aquitaine.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: We arrest your word.—

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Boyet, you can produce acquittances

speaker: PRINCESS
text: For such a sum from special officers

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Of Charles his father.

speaker: KING
text: Satisfy me so.

speaker: BOYET
text: So please your Grace, the packet is not come

speaker: BOYET
text: Where that and other specialties are bound.

speaker: BOYET
text: Tomorrow you shall have a sight of them.

speaker: KING
text: It shall suffice me; at which interview

speaker: KING
text: All liberal reason I will yield unto.

speaker: KING
text: Meantime receive such welcome at my hand

speaker: KING
text: As honor (without breach of honor) may

speaker: KING
text: Make tender of to thy true worthiness.

speaker: KING
text: You may not come, fair princess, within my gates,

speaker: KING
text: But here without you shall be so received

speaker: KING
text: As you shall deem yourself lodged in my heart,

speaker: KING
text: Though so denied fair harbor in my house.

speaker: KING
text: Your own good thoughts excuse me, and farewell.

speaker: KING
text: Tomorrow shall we visit you again.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Sweet health and fair desires consort your Grace.

speaker: KING
text: Thy own wish wish I thee in every place.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Lady, I will commend you to

speaker: BEROWNE
text: my own heart.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Pray you, do my commendations. I would

speaker: ROSALINE
text: be glad to see it.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I would you heard it groan.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Is the fool sick?

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Sick at the heart.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Alack, let it blood.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Would that do it good?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: My physic says “ay.”

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Will you prick ’t with your eye?

speaker: ROSALINE
text: No point, with my knife.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Now God save thy life.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: And yours from long living.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: I cannot stay thanksgiving.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: Sir, I pray you, a word. What lady is that same?

speaker: BOYET
text: The heir of Alanson, Katherine her name.

speaker: DUMAINE
text: A gallant lady, monsieur. Fare you well.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: I beseech you, a word. What is she in the white?

speaker: BOYET
text: A woman sometimes, an you saw her in the light.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: Perchance light in the light. I desire her name.

speaker: BOYET
text: She hath but one for herself; to desire that were a shame.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: Pray you, sir, whose daughter?

speaker: BOYET
text: Her mother’s, I have heard.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: God’s blessing on your beard!

speaker: BOYET
text: Good sir, be not offended. She is an heir of

speaker: BOYET
text: Falconbridge.

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: Nay, my choler is ended. She is a most

speaker: LONGAVILLE
text: sweet lady.

speaker: BOYET
text: Not unlike, sir, that may be.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: What’s her name in the cap?

speaker: BOYET
text: Rosaline, by good hap.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: Is she wedded or no?

speaker: BOYET
text: To her will, sir, or so.

speaker: BEROWNE
text: You are welcome, sir. Adieu.

speaker: BOYET
text: Farewell to me, sir, and welcome to you.

speaker: MARIA
text: That last is Berowne, the merry madcap lord.

speaker: MARIA
text: Not a word with him but a jest.

speaker: BOYET
text: And every jest but a word.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: It was well done of you to take him at his word.

speaker: BOYET
text: I was as willing to grapple as he was to board.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Two hot sheeps, marry.

speaker: BOYET
text: And wherefore not ships?

speaker: BOYET
text: No sheep, sweet lamb, unless we feed on your lips.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: You sheep and I pasture. Shall that finish the jest?

speaker: BOYET
text: So you grant pasture for me.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Not so, gentle beast,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: My lips are no common, though several they be.

speaker: BOYET
text: Belonging to whom?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To my fortunes and me.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Good wits will be jangling; but, gentles, agree,

speaker: PRINCESS
text: This civil war of wits were much better used

speaker: PRINCESS
text: On Navarre and his bookmen, for here ’tis abused.

speaker: BOYET
text: If my observation, which very seldom lies,

speaker: BOYET
text: By the heart’s still rhetoric, disclosèd wi’ th’ eyes,

speaker: BOYET
text: Deceive me not now, Navarre is infected.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: With what?

speaker: BOYET
text: With that which we lovers entitle “affected.”

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Your reason?

speaker: BOYET
text: Why, all his behaviors did make their retire

speaker: BOYET
text: To the court of his eye, peeping thorough desire.

speaker: BOYET
text: His heart like an agate with your print impressed,

speaker: BOYET
text: Proud with his form, in his eye pride expressed.

speaker: BOYET
text: His tongue, all impatient to speak and not see,

speaker: BOYET
text: Did stumble with haste in his eyesight to be;

speaker: BOYET
text: All senses to that sense did make their repair,

speaker: BOYET
text: To feel only looking on fairest of fair.

speaker: BOYET
text: Methought all his senses were locked in his eye,

speaker: BOYET
text: As jewels in crystal for some prince to buy,

speaker: BOYET
text: Who, tend’ring their own worth from where they were glassed,

speaker: BOYET
text: Did point you to buy them along as you passed.

speaker: BOYET
text: His face’s own margent did quote such amazes

speaker: BOYET
text: That all eyes saw his eyes enchanted with gazes.

speaker: BOYET
text: I’ll give you Aquitaine, and all that is his,

speaker: BOYET
text: An you give him for my sake but one loving kiss.

speaker: PRINCESS
text: Come, to our pavilion. Boyet is disposed.

speaker: BOYET
text: But to speak that in words which his eye hath disclosed.

speaker: BOYET
text: I only have made a mouth of his eye

speaker: BOYET
text: By adding a tongue which I know will not lie.

speaker: MARIA
text: Thou art an old lovemonger and speakest skillfully.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: He is Cupid’s grandfather, and learns news of him.

speaker: ROSALINE
text: Then was Venus like her mother, for her father is but grim.

speaker: BOYET
text: Do you hear, my mad wenches?

speaker: MARIA
text: No.

speaker: BOYET
text: What then, do you see?

speaker: MARIA
text: Ay, our way to be gone.

speaker: BOYET
text: You are too hard for me.
